old_claim.note: X-ray Crystallography provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Sanger completed insulin amino-acid sequencing chemically; X-ray crystallography determines three-dimensional structure and was not required for that sequence.

source_shape.source_locator: The historical review states that Sanger defined the complete structure and amino-acid sequence of insulin in 1955.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The historical review attributes the complete 1955 insulin amino-acid sequence to Sanger's protein-chemistry work.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The first complete sequence is independently documented without an X-ray method, so crystallography is a complementary structural technique rather than an origin dependency.

ontology_before: Protein sequencing treated X-ray crystallography as a necessary enabling lineage.
ontology_after: Protein sequencing is rooted in biochemistry; crystallographic structure determination remains a parallel method.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: protein_sequencing no longer directly depends on x_ray_crystallography.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to crystallography-assisted sequence inference.
A primary account shows Sanger required X-ray diffraction to determine insulin's amino-acid order.
short_reason: Sanger completed insulin amino-acid sequencing chemically; X-ray crystallography determines three-dimensional structure and was not required for that sequence.
